problem_text_cn:
## 一、(12分)

如图(a)所示，半径为$R$、折射率为$n<2$的透明琥珀球内，小虫$P$嵌在直径$AOB$中，靠近$B$端，与球心$O$相距$r$。
(1) 取$r=R/\sqrt{n}$，设想$P$是一个点光源，从球外观看，试求球面上被照亮的面积$S$。
(2) 取消$r=R/\sqrt{n}$假设，自然仍有$R>r>0$。令观察者从$A$端右方，沿直径$AOB$方向以较小的视角范围观看$P$，试求见到的$P$位置和真实的$P$位置相距多远？
数学参考公式：图(b)示的球冠(不含底圆面)面积为$S=2\pi Rh$。
problem_text_en:
## One. (12 points)

As shown in Fig. (a), inside a transparent amber sphere with radius $R$ and refractive index $n<2$, an insect $P$ is embedded on diameter $AOB$, near end $B$, at a distance $r$ from the center $O$.
(1) Assuming $r=R/\sqrt{n}$, consider $P$ as a point light source. From external observation, determine the illuminated area $S$ on the sphere's surface.
(2) Remove the assumption $r=R/\sqrt{n}$ while maintaining $R>r>0$. An observer views $P$ from the right side of end $A$ along the $AOB$ diameter with a small viewing angle. Calculate the positional deviation between the observed and actual locations of $P$.
Mathematical formula: The spherical‑cap area (excluding base) in Fig. (b) is $S=2\pi Rh$.
problem_solution_cn:
一、(12分)
(1) P点发出的一对正、反向光线，其光路和对应的几何参量，如题解图所示.其中$ \alpha $,$ \alpha $'间的关系为
$$ n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha^{\prime}. $$
全反射对应的入射角$ \alpha $临界值满足关系式：
$$ n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha^{\prime}|_{\alpha^{\prime}=\pi/2}=1,\quad\Rightarrow\quad\sin\alpha=\frac{1}{n}.\quad(2\text{分}) $$
此临界值对应的右侧$ \beta $角可由
$$ \frac{\sin\beta}{R}=\frac{\sin\alpha}{r},\quad\Rightarrow\quad\sin\beta=\frac{R}{r}\sin\alpha=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\quad(\text{取}\frac{\pi}{2}\geq\beta\geq0) $$
确定.对右侧区域，此时$ h_{\text{右}} $的计算式为
$$ h_{\text{右}}=R-R\cos(\alpha+\beta)=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta-\sin\alpha\sin\beta)],\quad(2\text{分}) $$
对左侧区域，此时$ h_{\text{左}} $的计算式为
$$ h_{\text{左}}=R-R\cos(\beta-\alpha)=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta+\sin\alpha\sin\beta)],\quad(2\text{分}) $$
球面被照亮的面积便为
$$ S=2\pi Rh_{\text{右}}+2\pi Rh_{\text{左}}=4\pi R^{2}(1-\cos\alpha\cos\beta). $$
将
$$ \sin\alpha=\frac{1}{n},\quad\Rightarrow\quad\cos\alpha=\frac{1}{n}\sqrt{n^{2}-1};\quad\sin\beta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}},\quad\Rightarrow\quad\cos\beta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n-1} $$
代入，得
$$ S=4\pi R^{2}\left(1-\frac{n-1}{n\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n+1}\right).\quad(2\text{分}) $$
(2)据凹球面近轴折射成像公式，得
$$ \frac{n}{u}+\frac{1}{v}=\frac{1-n}{-R};\quad u=R+r,\quad v\begin{cases}>0;\text{像在A点右侧},\\<0;\text{像在A点左侧},\end{cases}\quad(2\text{分}) $$
$$ \Rightarrow\quad v=-\frac{R(R+r)}{R-(n-1)r}<0;\text{像在A点左侧}.\quad(1\text{分}) $$
problem_solution_en:
One. (12 points)
(1) A pair of forward and backward light rays emitted from point P, their optical paths and corresponding geometric parameters are shown in the solution diagram. The relation between $\alpha$ and $\alpha'$ is
$$ n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha^{\prime}. $$
The critical incident angle $\alpha$ for total internal reflection satisfies
$$ n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha^{\prime}|_{\alpha^{\prime}=\pi/2}=1,\quad\Rightarrow\quad\sin\alpha=\frac{1}{n}.\quad(2\,\text{points}) $$
The corresponding angle $\beta$ on the right side is determined from
$$ \frac{\sin\beta}{R}=\frac{\sin\alpha}{r},\quad\Rightarrow\quad\sin\beta=\frac{R}{r}\sin\alpha=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\quad(\text{taking }\frac{\pi}{2}\geq\beta\geq0). $$
For the right region, the height $h_{\text{right}}$ is
$$ h_{\text{right}}=R-R\cos(\alpha+\beta)=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta-\sin\alpha\sin\beta)],\quad(2\,\text{points}) $$
For the left region, the height $h_{\text{left}}$ is
$$ h_{\text{left}}=R-R\cos(\beta-\alpha)=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta+\sin\alpha\sin\beta)],\quad(2\,\text{points}) $$
Thus the illuminated area on the sphere surface is
$$ S=2\pi Rh_{\text{right}}+2\pi Rh_{\text{left}}=4\pi R^{2}(1-\cos\alpha\cos\beta). $$
Substituting
$$ \sin\alpha=\frac{1}{n},\;\Rightarrow\;\cos\alpha=\frac{1}{n}\sqrt{n^{2}-1};\qquad\sin\beta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}},\;\Rightarrow\;\cos\beta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n-1} $$
gives
$$ S=4\pi R^{2}\left(1-\frac{n-1}{n\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n+1}\right).\quad(2\,\text{points}) $$
(2) Using the paraxial refraction formula for a concave spherical surface,
$$ \frac{n}{u}+\frac{1}{v}=\frac{1-n}{-R};\quad u=R+r,\quad v\begin{cases}>0;\text{image to the right of A},\\<0;\text{image to the left of A},\end{cases}\quad(2\,\text{points}) $$
$$ \Rightarrow\quad v=-\frac{R(R+r)}{R-(n-1)r}<0;\text{the image is to the left of A}.\quad(1\,\text{point}) $$
img_cn:
题目原图，编号为818_1.png，包含题目文字和两张示意子图。

顶部为题目文字：标题“一、(12分)”，之后是题目叙述，包含两个子问题和球冠面积公式提示。

左侧子图标记为“(a)”，是琥珀球的横截面示意图。图中：
- 圆形代表琥珀球横截面，标注中心O和半径R。
- 水平直径标记为AB，左端为A，右端为B。
- 小虫P位于OB段，靠近B端，用小黑点表示。
- 标注球心O到P的距离为r。
- 图中标注了折射率条件n<2。
- 文字描述了物理情景：半径R、折射率n的透明琥珀球，小虫P作为发光点。

右侧子图标记为“(b)”，是球冠面积示意图。图中：
- 圆形代表球体横截面。
- 标注球半径R和球冠高度h。
- 用虚线表示球冠的底面边界。
- 文字说明该图形为“球冠(不含底圆面)”，并给出面积公式S=2πRh。

两张子图均采用简洁的几何绘图风格，使用黑色线条绘制在白色背景上，关键参数用数学符号明确标注。

这是一张光学物理问题的解题过程图，编号为818_2.png。包含两个主要部分：几何光学分析和球面折射成像分析。

第一部分：几何光学分析。涉及全反射临界角计算和光照面积推导。包含一个光路图，图中标注了：发光点P、球心O、法线、入射角α/α'、折射角β等几何参量。光线路径显示从P点出发，在球面上发生折射。

第二部分：球面折射成像分析。通过凹球面折射公式进行符号推导，最终计算像点位置。图中公式采用LaTeX格式，包含三角函数、几何关系、光学定律等符号。

详细内容：
(1) 首先给出P点发出的正、反向光线光路及几何参量示意图，标明角度α与α'满足折射定律$n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha'$。
(2) 推导全反射临界角条件：$n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha'|_{\alpha'=\pi/2}=1$，得$\sin\alpha=1/n$。
(3) 由几何关系$\sin\beta/R=\sin\alpha/r$，结合$r=R/\sqrt{n}$，得$\sin\beta=1/\sqrt{n}$。
(4) 计算右侧区域球冠高度$h_{\text{右}}=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta-\sin\alpha\sin\beta)]$。
(5) 计算左侧区域球冠高度$h_{\text{左}}=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta+\sin\alpha\sin\beta)]$。
(6) 总照亮面积$S=2\pi Rh_{\text{右}}+2\pi Rh_{\text{左}}=4\pi R^{2}(1-\cos\alpha\cos\beta)$。
(7) 代入三角函数值化简得$S=4\pi R^{2}\left(1-\frac{n-1}{n\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n+1}\right)$。
(8) 对第二部分，应用凹球面近轴折射公式$n/u+1/v=(1-n)/(-R)$，物距$u=R+r$，解得像距$v=-R(R+r)/[R-(n-1)r]$，并判定$v<0$，即像在A点左侧。

页面底部有“2011年暑期物理竞赛辅导班联谊赛试题”字样。
img_en:
Original problem image, numbered 818_1.png, containing the problem text and two sub‑figures.

Top: problem text with title "One. (12 points)", followed by the problem statement including two sub‑questions and a hint for the spherical‑cap area formula.

Left sub‑figure labeled "(a)" is a cross‑sectional diagram of the amber sphere:
- Circle represents the sphere cross‑section, with center O and radius R marked.
- Horizontal diameter marked AB, with left end A, right end B.
- Insect P shown as a small black dot on segment OB, near B.
- Distance from center O to P labeled as r.
- Refractive index condition n<2 indicated.
- Text describes the physical situation: a transparent amber sphere of radius R, refractive index n, with insect P as a light source.

Right sub‑figure labeled "(b)" is a diagram illustrating the spherical‑cap area:
- Circle represents the sphere cross‑section.
- Sphere radius R and cap height h are marked.
- Dashed line indicates the cap's base boundary.
- Text states that the figure shows a "spherical cap (excluding base)" and gives the area formula S=2πRh.

Both sub‑figures are drawn in a clean geometric style with black lines on white background, key parameters labeled with mathematical symbols.

This is a solution diagram for an optical physics problem, numbered 818_2.png. It consists of two main parts: geometric optics analysis and spherical refraction imaging analysis.

Part 1: Geometric optics analysis. Involves calculation of critical angle for total internal reflection and derivation of the illuminated area. Contains a light‑path diagram labeling: light source P, center O, normal line, incident angle α/α', refraction angle β, etc. Light rays are shown leaving P and refracting at the sphere surface.

Part 2: Spherical refraction imaging analysis. Symbolic derivation using the concave spherical surface refraction formula, concluding with calculation of image position. Formulas are in LaTeX format, including trigonometric functions, geometric relations, and optical laws.

Detailed content:
(1) First, a schematic of forward and backward light rays from point P and the geometric parameters, indicating that angles α and α' satisfy the refraction law $n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha'$.
(2) Derivation of the total‑internal‑reflection critical‑angle condition: $n\sin\alpha=\sin\alpha'|_{\alpha'=\pi/2}=1$, giving $\sin\alpha=1/n$.
(3) From the geometric relation $\sin\beta/R=\sin\alpha/r$, together with $r=R/\sqrt{n}$, obtain $\sin\beta=1/\sqrt{n}$.
(4) Compute the spherical‑cap height for the right region: $h_{\text{right}}=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta-\sin\alpha\sin\beta)]$.
(5) Compute the spherical‑cap height for the left region: $h_{\text{left}}=R[1-(\cos\alpha\cos\beta+\sin\alpha\sin\beta)]$.
(6) Total illuminated area: $S=2\pi Rh_{\text{right}}+2\pi Rh_{\text{left}}=4\pi R^{2}(1-\cos\alpha\cos\beta)$.
(7) Substitute trigonometric values and simplify to $S=4\pi R^{2}\left(1-\frac{n-1}{n\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n+1}\right)$.
(8) For the second part, apply the paraxial refraction formula for a concave spherical surface: $n/u+1/v=(1-n)/(-R)$, with object distance $u=R+r$, solve for image distance $v=-R(R+r)/[R-(n-1)r]$, and conclude $v<0$, i.e., the image lies to the left of point A.

Bottom of the page bears the text "2011年暑期物理竞赛辅导班联谊赛试题" (2011 Summer Physics Competition Tutorial Class Friendship Test Questions).
problem_answer:
(1) 球面上被照亮的面积 $S=4\pi R^{2}\left(1-\dfrac{n-1}{n\sqrt{n}}\sqrt{n+1}\right)$。
(2) 见到的$P$位置与真实的$P$位置相距 $\Delta = \left|v\right| = \dfrac{R(R+r)}{R-(n-1)r}$，其中$v<0$表示像在$A$点左侧。
